reasoning:
The reference class is a large-cap stock forecasting over a very short horizon (1 week to 1 month). Changes in stock prices over such short periods approximate a random walk, with typically 50-55% probability of increasing due to long-term equity premiums, but driven mostly by noise or short-term momentum. Given the historical data, the stock ($IT) dropped enormously after an early February 2026 earnings report (from ~$242 to $152), bottomed around $144 on Feb 23, and has shown strong upward momentum since, closing at 157.20 on Feb 27. The target value is the closing price on 2026-03-01, but March 1 is a Sunday, so its "market close price on 2026-03-01" will be the previous close, which is 157.20 from Friday, Feb 27. The forecast questions whether the stock will be higher on two specific resolution dates: March 8 and March 31.
1. March 8: Exactly one week later. Short terms tend to carry momentum. The stock recently bounced rapidly upward (4 up days in a row). Given there are no dividends that would cause mechanical drops (Gartner doesn't pay one) and earnings were freshly released in Feb, the event space for March 8 is driven by momentum. Linear predictions estimate a ~49% chance, but standard quantitative momentum predicts short-term continuation. I estimate a 56% probability.
2. March 31: A full month out. Over 1 month out, momentum fades and it gets closer to a 50/50 coin flip plus standard stock drift. The stock is volatile right now (trying to establish a new base after a crash). I'll predict 53%, letting uncertainty increase by pulling the probability closer to 50%.
